steps.title: Translate the English inequality into algebraic form
steps.content:
The phrase x is at most 5 less than 4 times the value of y breaks down as:
  • at most means $\leq$
  • 5 less than 4 times the value of y means $4y - 5$
Combining these: $$x \leq 4y - 5$$

steps.title: Substitute y = -3 and find the maximum value of x
steps.content:
Plugging in $y = -3$ into the inequality:
$$x \leq 4(-3) - 5$$
$$x \leq -12 - 5$$
$$x \leq -17$$
Since $x$ can be at most $-17$, the greatest possible value of $x$ is $-17$.
